Bangus.description: A silvery, torpedo-shaped fish widely farmed across the Philippines and considered its national fish. It thrives in both freshwater and saltwater environments, making it ideal for aquaculture. Known for its mild flavor and tender flesh, though notably bony.
health_risks: Moderate mercury level — limit to 1-2 servings per week
Relatively high in fat compared to leaner fish

Tilapia.description: A freshwater fish native to Africa and widely farmed across the Philippines. It has white, mild-flavored, lean flesh with a firm texture. One of the most commercially important aquaculture species in the country.
health_risks: High omega-6 to omega-3 ratio may promote inflammation
Low omega-3 content compared to other fish

Big Head Carp.description: A large freshwater fish introduced to the Philippines from mainland Asia for aquaculture. Widely farmed in Laguna Lake alongside tilapia and milkfish. Known for rapid growth and mild-flavored white flesh, though it has a notably large head and many small bones.
health_risks: Moderate omega-6 content may offset some omega-3 benefits
Lower omega-3 levels compared to marine fish
